column1: #   decomposition -- effort is model-coupled (Haiku@max != Opus@max as a multiplier)
#   separability is a false assumption and is not used.
#   rel_cost(node) = product of MEASURED same-task ratios along the path linking it to the base;
#   several paths -> MEDIAN of the products. Edges = same-task ratios (2 models @same effort
#   or 2 efforts @same model). CI = spread of the concurrent paths (multiplicative).
#   A node with no measured edge is NOT filled by a generic effort rule: it is either n/a
#   flagged 'inferred' with a widened band. One model's effort shape is NEVER a template for another.
# conf: HIGH (>=3 indep same-task paths agree) | MED | LOW/WIDE | INFER (no direct edge
# --- WELL-MEASURED ANCHOR NODES (direct same-task edges to base) ---
# @medium cross-model: fable-5 1.95[1.7-2.1]HIGH | opus-4.8 1.00 | opus-4.7 0.95[0.6-1.4]LOW | sonnet-5 1.05[0.7-1.6]WIDE-tasksize | sonnet-4.6 0.60[0.5-0.75]HIGH | haiku-4.5 0.19[0.11-0.22]HIGH
# opus-4.8 effort sweep (own measured path): low 0.70 | medium 1.00 | high 1.25 | xhigh 1.70 | max 2.30 [osworld/zenn/qiita]
# NB: effort->QUALITY is ~flat/inverted-U on coding (max vs low = +0.3-1.3pp); effort->COST can be NON-MONOTONIC on agentic-with-retries (higher effort sometimes cheaper: HAL).
# NB2: non-Opus cells at non-medium efforts marked INFER below = no published per-model effort sweep;
#   estimate carries the Opus effort *ballpark* only as a prior

column1: # AA-Index(max): fable60 opus4.8-56 opus4.7-54 sonnet5-53 sonnet4.6-47
#  task_type: coding/agentic -> effort-quality FLAT (plateau); reasoning-math -> effort PAYS (+9-29%); computer-use -> costs 10-50x higher absolute.
#  complexity: easy -> effort~flat cost; hard -> effort ~2x; harder still -> inverted-U (overthinking hurts).
#  caching: ~90-96% cache-read on agentic; broken cache can 2.7x cross-model cost.
